eyebrow: Music Palette · Sourced from scene & sleeve
tagline: Forty-eight scenes, read for their colour, not their logo.
metaNote:
Color sourced from scene,
sleeve art & venue light
sourcing: Every palette is sampled from a specific genre, era, or scene's visual world — its record-sleeve design, its venue and stage light, its fashion, and the materials of its instruments. The aim is the colour the music looks like, not a cliché shorthand for the genre.

volumes.title: Jazz
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol I · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from jazz, smoke, brass, and cool blue.
preface: Jazz colour runs from the cool blue-and-cream of mid-century record sleeves to the smoke-and-brass warmth of the late-night club. The palette is sophisticated, warm-metal and cool-shadow, with one bright accent.
Each refuses a flat 'old jazz sepia': the music's design language is bold blue, warm brass, and graphic contrast.

palettes.kicker: Cool jazz · the mid-century record sleeve
palettes.title: A modernist album-cover palette of cool blue, cream, and bold typographic accent
palettes.source: Color from the sleeve aesthetic: cool cobalt blue, cream paper, charcoal photo-grey, a graphic red, and a warm gold.

d.text: Cool cobalt blue leads the sleeve.

s.text: Cream stock, charcoal photo, and teal type.

a.text: Graphic red and warm gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded sepia nostalgia. The jazz sleeve is crisp cobalt-and-cream with bold type — modernist graphic, not a faded old photo.

palettes.kicker: The late-night club · the smoky set
palettes.title: A dim basement club of brass-instrument gold, red velvet, and cigarette smoke
palettes.source: Color from the club: brass-saxophone gold, red-velvet seat, smoke-grey air, spotlight warm, and a deep shadow black.

swatches.name: Brass instrument, gold
swatches.note: The sax and trumpet under the light.

d.text: Deep black-brown shadow is the club's field.

s.text: Brass gold, red velvet, and smoke grey.

a.text: Amber spotlight and piano black as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright clean stage. The jazz club is smoky shadow lit by brass-gold and one amber spot — warmth in the dark, not a lit room.

palettes.kicker: New Orleans brass · the street parade
palettes.title: A second-line brass band in the sun, gold horns, white shirts, and parasol colour
palettes.source: Color from the parade: brass-horn gold, white-shirt cream, sousaphone brass, parasol purple-green-gold, and a deep shadow.

d.text: Warm brass-gold leads the parade.

s.text: White shirts, carnival purple, and street shadow.

a.text: Parasol green and Mardi-Gras gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted vintage band. The second-line is sunlit brass-gold with carnival purple, green, and gold — vivid celebration, not a faded photo.

palettes.title: A modal-jazz mood of deep blue, midnight teal, brass, and smoke
palettes.source: Color from the modal session: midnight blue, teal shadow, brass gold, smoke grey, and a warm spotlight.

d.text: Midnight blue leads the modal mood.

s.text: Teal shadow, brass gold, and smoke grey.

a.text: Amber spot and bass wood as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded sepia. Modal jazz is deep blue and teal warmed by brass — a cool-blue mood, not a brown nostalgia.

volumes.title: Punk
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol II · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from punk, black, ransom-note, and shock.
preface: Punk's palette is confrontational — black leather and denim, the ransom-note collage of black-and-white photocopy, with one shock of pink, red, or yellow. The colour is DIY, high-contrast, and aggressive.
Each refuses a tasteful muted scheme: punk is black, white, and a single screaming accent.

palettes.title: A photocopied zine aesthetic of black, newsprint, and shock-pink cut-out letters
palettes.source: Color from the DIY sleeve: photocopy black, newsprint grey-white, shock pink, safety-yellow, and a smeared red.

d.text: Photocopy black is the sleeve's field.

s.text: Newsprint grey-white, shock pink, and halftone grey.

a.text: Acid yellow and blood red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Tasteful muted scheme. Punk is black and newsprint detonated by shock-pink and acid-yellow — confrontation, not good taste.

palettes.title: A sweaty punk club of black leather, chrome studs, and red stage light
palettes.source: Color from the club: leather black, chrome-stud silver, denim blue, red stage light, and a bleach-blond.

d.text: Leather black is the club's dominant field.

s.text: Chrome studs, faded denim, and grey-black dark.

a.text: Red stage light and bleach hair as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Colourful stage show. The punk club is black leather and chrome under one red wash — stark and hard, not a lit-up gig.

palettes.title: A '90s feminist-punk zine of red, black, hand-scrawl, and photocopied pink
palettes.source: Color from the zine: scrawl red, photocopy black, marker-pink, newsprint white, and a highlighter teal.

d.text: Marker red leads the zine collage.

s.text: Photocopy black, newsprint white, and marker pink.

a.text: Highlighter teal and tape beige as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Polished design. Riot-grrrl zines are raw red, black, and marker-pink on newsprint — handmade and loud, not a clean layout.

palettes.title: A 2000s pop-punk palette of black, hot pink, and skate-deck colour
palettes.source: Color from pop-punk: black tee, hot pink, checker white, skate-green, and a highlighter yellow.

d.text: Black tee leads the pop-punk look.

s.text: Hot pink, checker white, and skate green.

a.text: Highlighter yellow and silver as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted subtle palette. Pop-punk is black with hot-pink and highlighter brights — loud and youthful, not understated.

volumes.title: Disco & Funk
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol III · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from disco, mirror-ball, gold, and glitter.
preface: Disco and funk colour is pure glamour — mirror-ball silver, glitter gold, hot pink and purple light, against a dark dancefloor. The palette is luminous, metallic, and saturated.
Each refuses a muted retro-brown '70s look: disco is silver, gold, and saturated club light, not earth tones.

palettes.title: A glittering nightclub of mirror-ball silver, gold lamé, and pink-purple light
palettes.source: Color from the club: mirror-ball silver, gold-lamé, hot-pink light, dancefloor purple, and a deep black.

swatches.name: Dancefloor dark, black-purple
swatches.note: The dark club the lights play on.

d.text: Dark black-purple is the dancefloor field.

s.text: Mirror-ball silver, gold lamé, and hot-pink light.

a.text: Electric purple and cyan beams as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted '70s brown. Disco is silver, gold, and saturated pink-purple light on black — glittering glamour, not earth tones.

palettes.title: A '70s music-show stage of rainbow logo light, afro silhouettes, and bold colour
palettes.source: Color from the show: rainbow-orange, sunshine yellow, grass green, sky blue, and a hot magenta.

d.text: Warm logo orange leads the stage.

s.text: Sunshine yellow, grass green, and sky blue.

a.text: Hot magenta and stage black as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted vintage TV. The show is a full saturated rainbow — orange, yellow, green, blue — joyous pop colour, not a faded screen.

palettes.title: A psychedelic-funk cosmos of metallic purple, chrome, star-gold, and neon
palettes.source: Color from the mothership: cosmic purple, chrome silver, star gold, neon green, and a deep space black.

d.text: Cosmic purple leads the funk cosmos.

s.text: Chrome silver, star gold, and space black.

a.text: Neon green and hot pink as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Earthy soul brown. P-Funk's art is a chrome-and-neon cosmos — purple, silver, and gold, a space fantasy, not earth tones.

palettes.title: A roller-disco rink of neon-stripe brights, blond wood floor, and disco light
palettes.source: Color from the rink: skate-stripe orange, rink-wood tan, neon pink, electric blue, and a chrome wheel.

d.text: Skate-stripe orange leads the rink.

s.text: Blond wood, neon pink, and electric blue.

a.text: Chrome wheel and purple light as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted retro brown. The roller rink is neon brights on blond wood — saturated fun, not a faded '70s tone.

volumes.title: Reggae & Dub
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol IV · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from reggae, red-gold-green, smoke, and sun.
preface: Reggae and dub colour centres on the Rastafarian red, gold, and green, set against Kingston sun, dub-studio smoke, and the black of vinyl. The palette is warm, earthy, and symbolic.
Each refuses a generic tropical brightness: this is specific Rasta tricolour and Jamaican-street warmth, not a beach postcard.

palettes.title: The red, gold, and green of roots reggae against black and lion imagery
palettes.source: Color from roots reggae: Rasta green, gold-yellow, red, lion-black, and a warm earth brown.

d.text: Rasta green leads the roots palette.

s.text: Gold yellow, deep red, and lion black.

a.text: Earth brown and parchment cream as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Generic tropical neon. Roots reggae is the symbolic red-gold-green tricolour with black and earth — meaningful, not a beach palette.

palettes.title: A dancehall yard at dusk: speaker-stack black, sodium light, and corrugated tin
palettes.source: Color from the yard: speaker black, sodium-light amber, corrugated-tin grey-blue, dub-smoke haze, and a neon-green strip.

d.text: Speaker-stack black is the yard's field.

s.text: Sodium amber, tin grey-blue, and smoke haze.

a.text: Neon green and dusk blue as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright sunny beach. The sound-system yard is dark and sodium-lit, hazed with smoke — a night scene, not a postcard.

palettes.title: A dark dub studio of glowing VU meters, tape reels, and reverb-green light
palettes.source: Color from the studio: desk black, VU-meter amber, tape-reel brown, reverb-green glow, and a warm lamp.

d.text: Matte-black desk is the studio's field.

s.text: Amber VU, tape brown, and grey-black dark.

a.text: Green LED and warm lamp as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright clean studio. The dub studio is dark, lit by amber meters and green LEDs — analogue glow, not a white room.

palettes.title: A mellow lovers-rock party of warm red light, deep blue, and gold
palettes.source: Color from the blues dance: warm red light, deep-blue dark, gold chain, brown skin-warmth, and a green accent.

d.text: Warm red light leads the basement dance.

s.text: Deep blue dark, gold chain, and brown shadow.

a.text: Black vinyl and green LED as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright party colour. Lovers rock is intimate red-and-blue light in the dark — mellow warmth, not a bright disco.

volumes.title: Hip-Hop
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol V · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from hip-hop, concrete, gold, and spray-paint.
preface: Hip-hop colour spans the grey concrete and gold chains of golden-age New York, the spray-paint brights of graffiti, and the moody purples of Southern trap. The palette is urban, bold, and accent-gold.
Each refuses a generic 'urban grey': hip-hop's visual language is vivid graffiti colour and gleaming gold against the concrete.

palettes.title: A '90s New York palette of concrete grey, gold chain, and subway brick
palettes.source: Color from the era: concrete grey, chain gold, subway-brick red, denim blue, and a black brim.

d.text: Concrete grey is the era's field.

s.text: Chain gold, brick red, and denim blue.

a.text: Black brim and wheat boot as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Flat monochrome grey. Golden-age hip-hop is concrete lifted by gleaming gold and brick red — warm accents, not all grey.

palettes.title: A wildstyle spray-paint burner in chrome, fill-colour, and black outline
palettes.source: Color from the piece: spray magenta, chrome silver, electric blue, fill yellow, and a black outline.

d.text: Spray magenta leads the burner.

s.text: Chrome silver, electric blue, and spray yellow.

a.text: Black outline and car steel as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted street tones. Graffiti is full spray-can saturation — magenta, blue, yellow, chrome — maximal colour, by design.

palettes.title: A moody Atlanta-trap palette of deep purple, codeine, and gold grill
palettes.source: Color from the trap aesthetic: deep purple, lean-purple haze, gold grill, night black, and a strip-club neon pink.

d.text: Deep trap purple leads the palette.

s.text: Black-purple night, gold grill, and violet haze.

a.text: Hot-pink neon and cyan dash as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright daytime colour. Trap visuals are deep purple and night-black with gold and neon — a nocturnal mood, not daylight.

palettes.title: A G-funk palette of sunset gold, lowrider candy-paint, palm silhouette, and chrome
palettes.source: Color from G-funk: sunset gold, candy-paint purple, palm-silhouette black, sky pink, and chrome.

d.text: Sunset gold leads the G-funk palette.

s.text: Candy-purple, sunset pink, and palm black.

a.text: Chrome rim and gold chain as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Grey urban grit. G-funk is warm sunset gold and candy-paint purple — a sunny lowrider mood, not a grey street.

volumes.title: Electronic & Rave
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol VI · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from electronic music, acid, laser, and concrete.
preface: Electronic music colour runs from the acid-house smiley yellow to Detroit-techno chrome, Berlin-club concrete grey, and the full-spectrum lasers of a rave. The palette is synthetic, high-energy, and light-based.
Each refuses a muted ambient palette: club electronic is saturated laser colour and acid brights against the dark.

palettes.title: A late-'80s rave flyer of acid-yellow smiley, black, and day-glo
palettes.source: Color from acid house: acid yellow, smiley black, day-glo orange, electric purple, and a hot pink.

d.text: Acid yellow leads the flyer.

s.text: Smiley black, day-glo orange, and electric purple.

a.text: Hot pink and acid green as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Tasteful muted scheme. Acid house is full fluorescent saturation — acid yellow, day-glo orange, hot pink — maximal, not subtle.

palettes.title: A futurist techno palette of chrome silver, black, and electric-blue circuitry
palettes.source: Color from Detroit techno: chrome silver, machine black, circuit-blue, steel grey, and a warning-red LED.

d.text: Machine black is the techno field.

s.text: Chrome silver, circuit blue, and steel grey.

a.text: Warning-red LED and cyan as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Warm organic palette. Detroit techno is cold chrome-and-black with electric-blue circuitry — machine futurism, not warmth.

palettes.title: A dark warehouse rave cut by green lasers, strobe white, and UV purple
palettes.source: Color from the rave: warehouse black, laser green, strobe white, UV purple, and a haze-lit cyan.

swatches.name: Warehouse dark, black
swatches.note: The dark the lights cut through.

d.text: Warehouse black is the field the lasers cut.

s.text: Laser green, UV purple, and haze grey-blue.

a.text: Strobe white and cyan beam as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Lit ambient room. The rave is near-total black sliced by saturated laser green and UV — light-on-dark, not an ambient glow.

palettes.title: A vaporwave palette of pastel pink, cyan, purple gradient, and Roman-bust white
palettes.source: Color from vaporwave: pastel pink, cyan, gradient purple, statue white, and a grid magenta.

d.text: Pastel vapor pink leads the aesthetic.

s.text: Pastel cyan, gradient purple, and statue white.

a.text: Grid magenta and sun gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted natural palette. Vaporwave is candy pastel pink-and-cyan with neon grid — a digital dream, not a natural scheme.

volumes.title: Metal
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol VII · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from metal, black, blood, and cold silver.
preface: Metal's palette is dark and dramatic — black leather and night, blood red, cold corpse-paint white and silver, and the deep green or blue of atmospheric subgenres. The colour is theatrical and high-contrast.
Each refuses an all-black void: even metal uses one charged accent — blood red, frost blue, corpse white — against the dark.

palettes.kicker: Black metal · the forest at night
palettes.title: A frostbitten black-metal scene of corpse-paint white, night-black, and cold blue
palettes.source: Color from black metal: night black, corpse-paint white, frost-blue, dead-tree grey, and a blood red.

d.text: Night black is the dominant field.

s.text: Corpse-paint white, frost blue, and dead-tree grey.

a.text: Blood red and pale moon as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Total featureless black. Black metal is black set against stark corpse-white and frost-blue — high contrast, not a void.

palettes.title: A heavy doom palette of deep purple, amber-fuzz, and smoke against black
palettes.source: Color from doom: deep purple, amber-fuzz gold, smoke grey, amp black, and a blood-orange.

d.text: Deep doom purple leads the haze.

s.text: Amp black, fuzz amber, and smoke grey.

a.text: Blood orange and dim red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright clean rock. Doom is deep purple and amp-black hazed with amber fuzz — heavy and warm-dark, not a bright stage.

palettes.kicker: Power metal · the fantasy album art
palettes.title: An epic fantasy-metal scene of steel blue, gold, dragon-fire orange, and sky
palettes.source: Color from power metal: steel blue, armour silver, gold, dragon-fire orange, and a stormy sky purple.

d.text: Steel blue leads the fantasy scene.

s.text: Armour silver, gold, and storm-purple sky.

a.text: Dragon-fire orange and magic cyan as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Grim monochrome black. Power metal art is epic colour — steel blue, gold, and dragon-fire — heroic fantasy, not all-black.

palettes.kicker: Symphonic & gothic metal · the cathedral set
palettes.title: A gothic-metal scene of deep crimson, black, candle gold, and cathedral stone
palettes.source: Color from gothic metal: deep crimson, black velvet, candle gold, stone grey, and a moon-silver.

d.text: Deep crimson leads the gothic scene.

s.text: Black velvet, candle gold, and stone grey.

a.text: Moon silver and stained-glass violet as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Flat black void. Gothic metal is deep crimson and candle-gold against black and stone — theatrical, not a featureless dark.

volumes.title: Country & Folk
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol VIII · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from country & folk, denim, wheat, and rhinestone.
preface: Country and folk colour is warm Americana — denim blue, wheat and dust gold, weathered barn-wood, with the occasional flash of rhinestone-suit colour. The palette is earthy, nostalgic, and sun-worn.
Each refuses a garish modern country-pop palette: this is denim, dust, and wood, with rhinestone as the one bright note.

palettes.title: A weathered folk palette of denim blue, wheat gold, and dust-brown
palettes.source: Color from folk Americana: denim blue, wheat gold, dust brown, barn-wood grey, and a faded-flag red.

d.text: Worn denim blue leads the folk palette.

s.text: Wheat gold, dust brown, and barn grey.

a.text: Faded-flag red and pale sky as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Slick country-pop colour. Folk Americana is denim, wheat, and dust — weathered and warm, not a glossy stage palette.

palettes.title: A classic-country stage of rhinestone-suit embroidery, red curtain, and gold
palettes.source: Color from the Opry: rhinestone-turquoise suit, embroidery rose, red-velvet curtain, suit cream, and a gold trim.

d.text: Rhinestone turquoise leads the suit.

s.text: Rose embroidery, red curtain, and cream suit.

a.text: Gold trim and warm spotlight as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Drab denim only. The Opry rhinestone suit is turquoise, rose, and gold against red velvet — flamboyant showbiz, not workwear.

palettes.title: An Appalachian porch jam of warm wood, instrument varnish, and green hills
palettes.source: Color from the porch: instrument-varnish amber, porch-wood brown, banjo-head cream, hill green, and a denim blue.

d.text: Amber instrument varnish leads the porch.

s.text: Porch-wood brown, banjo cream, and hill green.

a.text: Denim blue and amber jar as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold modern palette. The bluegrass porch is warm varnish, wood, and hill-green — homespun warmth, not a sleek stage.

palettes.title: An outlaw-country palette of dust, denim-black, sunset red, and worn leather
palettes.source: Color from outlaw country: desert dust tan, black denim, sunset red, leather brown, and a turquoise-bolo.

d.text: Desert-dust tan leads the outlaw palette.

s.text: Black denim, sunset red, and leather brown.

a.text: Turquoise bolo and silver buckle as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Shiny Nashville pop. Outlaw country is dust, black, and worn leather with a turquoise note — rugged, not a glossy stage.

volumes.title: Classical & Opera
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol IX · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from the concert hall, gilt, velvet, and candle.
preface: Classical and operatic colour is the grandeur of the hall — gilt and red velvet, the black-and-white of the orchestra, the warm wood of instruments, and candlelit baroque gold. The palette is rich, formal, and warm.
Each refuses a cold austere palette: the concert hall is warm gilt, deep velvet, and instrument amber.

palettes.kicker: The opera house · the gilded auditorium
palettes.title: A horseshoe of red-velvet boxes and gilt under a great chandelier
palettes.source: Color from the house: red-velvet, gilt gold, chandelier crystal, ceiling-fresco blue, and a deep box shadow.

d.text: Deep red velvet is the auditorium's field.

s.text: Gilt gold, crystal white, and box shadow.

a.text: Fresco blue and cream programme as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Austere modern hall. The opera house is red velvet and gilt under crystal — warm grandeur, not a grey concrete auditorium.

palettes.title: A symphony platform of black-and-white dress, warm string-wood, and brass
palettes.source: Color from the platform: formal black, white-shirt, string-instrument amber, brass gold, and a warm stage-wood.

swatches.name: Shirt front, white
swatches.note: The white shirts and music sheets.

d.text: Formal black is the platform's field.

s.text: White shirts, string amber, and stage wood.

a.text: Brass gold and warm light as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold blue concert grade. The orchestra is black-and-white warmed by string-amber and brass — formal warmth, not a cold stage.

palettes.title: A baroque music room of candle gold, deep wood, harpsichord paint, and damask
palettes.source: Color from the chamber: candle gold, deep-wood brown, harpsichord-green paint, damask red, and a gilt detail.

d.text: Warm candle gold leads the chamber.

s.text: Deep wood, harpsichord green, and damask red.

a.text: Gilt leaf and cream score as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Bright even lighting. The baroque chamber glows by candle gold against deep wood — warm shadow, not a flat-lit room.

palettes.title: A Romantic-era recital of warm candlelight, rosewood piano, ivory, and deep red
palettes.source: Color from the recital: candle gold, rosewood-piano brown, ivory-key cream, deep-red drape, and a brass-lamp.

swatches.name: Ivory keys, cream
swatches.note: The piano keys and sheet music.

d.text: Warm candle gold leads the recital.

s.text: Rosewood piano, ivory keys, and red drape.

a.text: Brass lamp and brown shadow as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold bright stage. The Romantic recital glows by candle gold on rosewood and ivory — intimate warmth, not a lit hall.

volumes.title: Psychedelia & the '60s
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol X · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from psychedelia, liquid light and op-art.
preface: Psychedelic '60s colour is the liquid light-show and the concert poster — swirling complementary brights, op-art contrast, and the warm orange-and-pink of the Summer of Love. The palette is vibrant, swirling, and clashing-by-design.
Each refuses a muted vintage wash: psychedelia is maximal saturated colour, deliberately vibrating.

palettes.kicker: The Fillmore poster · the concert handbill
palettes.title: A swirling op-art gig poster of clashing orange, magenta, and teal lettering
palettes.source: Color from the poster: vibrating orange, magenta, teal, lime, and a deep purple.

swatches.name: Poster orange, vibrating
swatches.note: The warm half of the clash.

d.text: Vibrating orange leads the poster.

s.text: Hot magenta, electric teal, and acid lime.

a.text: Deep purple and gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted vintage tones. The psychedelic poster is maximally saturated clashing colour designed to vibrate, not a faded print.

palettes.kicker: Liquid light show · the projected oil-wheel
palettes.title: A wet-projected light show of blooming magenta, amber, and blue oil blobs
palettes.source: Color from the projection: blooming magenta, oil amber, deep blue, emerald, and a hot red.

d.text: Magenta oil-bloom leads the projection.

s.text: Amber, deep blue, and projection dark.

a.text: Emerald and hot red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Pale washed colour. The liquid light show is saturated blooming oil colour on black — vivid and wet, not a faded wash.

palettes.kicker: Summer of Love · the Haight-Ashbury palette
palettes.title: A flower-power scene of warm orange, paisley pink, sky blue, and grass green
palettes.source: Color from '67: flower-power orange, paisley pink, sky blue, grass green, and a sunny yellow.

swatches.name: Flower-power orange, warm
swatches.note: The warm dominant of the era.

d.text: Flower-power orange leads the era.

s.text: Paisley pink, sky blue, and grass green.

a.text: Sunny yellow and deep purple as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded hippie sepia. The Summer of Love is warm saturated flower-power colour — orange, pink, blue, green — joyous, not faded.

palettes.kicker: Mod & British Invasion · the op-art club
palettes.title: A '60s mod palette of target red-white-blue, op-art black-white, and scooter chrome
palettes.source: Color from mod: target red, navy blue, op-art white, op-art black, and scooter chrome.

d.text: Target red leads the mod palette.

s.text: Navy blue, op-art white, and op-art black.

a.text: Scooter chrome and mustard gold as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Faded vintage wash. Mod is crisp target red-white-blue and op-art black-white — sharp graphic pop, not a faded retro tone.

volumes.title: K-Pop & J-Pop
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol XI · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from idol pop, candy pastel and neon MV.
preface: K-pop and J-pop visual design is hyper-produced candy colour — pastel idol concepts, neon music-video sets, and Harajuku clash. The palette is bright, sweet, and saturated, switching between soft pastel and electric neon.
Each refuses a muted indie palette: idol pop is deliberate candy brightness, pastel or neon, never understated.

palettes.kicker: Pastel idol concept · the debut MV
palettes.title: A soft idol-pop concept of cotton-candy pink, mint, lilac, and cream
palettes.source: Color from the pastel concept: cotton-candy pink, mint green, lilac, butter cream, and a sky blue.

d.text: Cotton-candy pink leads the pastel concept.

s.text: Mint green, lilac, and butter cream.

a.text: Pale sky blue and cherry red as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted indie palette. The idol concept is full candy pastel — pink, mint, lilac — sweet and bright, not understated.

palettes.title: A high-energy neon music-video set of magenta, cyan, and electric purple
palettes.source: Color from the neon set: magenta, electric cyan, neon purple, hot yellow, and a deep black.

swatches.name: Set black, dark
swatches.note: The dark stage the neon pops on.

d.text: Neon magenta leads the MV set.

s.text: Electric cyan, set black, and neon purple.

a.text: Hot yellow and neon green as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Soft natural light. The neon MV is saturated magenta-and-cyan on black — electric and high-contrast, not naturally lit.

palettes.title: A decora street-fashion riot of clashing pink, turquoise, yellow, and toy colour
palettes.source: Color from Harajuku: clashing pink, turquoise, lemon yellow, purple, and a candy red.

d.text: Decora pink leads the street clash.

s.text: Bright turquoise, lemon yellow, and purple.

a.text: Candy red and pale mint as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Coordinated muted palette. Harajuku decora is a deliberate clash of bright pink, turquoise, and yellow — maximalist, not tasteful.

palettes.title: A Japanese city-pop palette of sunset gradient, neon, palm, and chrome
palettes.source: Color from city pop: sunset magenta-orange, ocean teal, neon cyan, palm-night purple, and chrome.

d.text: Magenta-orange sunset leads city pop.

s.text: Ocean teal, neon cyan, and palm-night purple.

a.text: Chrome and hot pink as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted retro tone. City pop is a saturated sunset gradient with neon and chrome — glossy '80s glamour, not a faded photo.

volumes.title: Blues & Soul
volumes.eyebrow: Music · Vol XII · Sourced from Scene & Sleeve
volumes.h1: Four palettes from blues & soul, juke-joint, Motown, and Stax.
preface: Blues and soul colour is warm and human — the smoky amber of a juke joint, the polished glamour of Motown, the earthy red-and-yellow of Stax, and the deep blue of the Delta night. The palette is warm, soulful, and grounded.
Each refuses a cold modern grade: this is a warm world of amber light, gold suits, and deep blue, with one bright accent.

palettes.title: A Mississippi juke joint of warm amber light, dark wood, and a deep blue night
palettes.source: Color from the joint: juke amber, dark-wood brown, Delta-night blue, neon-beer red, and a guitar-gold.

d.text: Warm juke amber leads the joint.

s.text: Dark wood, Delta-night blue, and smoke.

a.text: Neon-beer red and steel-gold guitar as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold blue grade. The juke joint is warm amber and dark wood against a blue night — intimate warmth, not a cold modern bar.

palettes.title: A polished Motown revue of gold lamé gowns, deep red curtain, and spotlight
palettes.source: Color from the revue: gold-lamé, red-curtain velvet, spotlight warm-white, stage black, and a sequin teal.

d.text: Gold lamé leads the Motown stage.

s.text: Red-velvet curtain, warm spotlight, and black stage.

a.text: Teal sequin and bronze skin as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Muted retro brown. Motown is glamorous gold and deep red under a warm spot — polished showbiz, not a faded photo.

palettes.title: A Memphis-soul palette of warm red, mustard yellow, and finger-snap graphics
palettes.source: Color from the Stax aesthetic: soul red, mustard yellow, warm cream, deep brown, and a turquoise accent.

d.text: Warm soul red leads the Stax palette.

s.text: Mustard yellow, warm cream, and deep brown.

a.text: Turquoise and warm orange as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold graphic palette. Stax soul is warm red, mustard, and brown — earthy Southern warmth, not a cool modern scheme.

palettes.title: A gospel-choir scene of robe purple, gold trim, warm wood, and stained-glass light
palettes.source: Color from the gospel service: choir-robe purple, gold trim, warm-wood pew, stained-glass colour, and a white collar.

d.text: Deep robe purple leads the choir.

s.text: Gold trim, warm pew wood, and blue glass.

a.text: Red glass and white collar as small reads.

palettes.refuses: Cold plain palette. Gospel is rich robe-purple and gold warmed by wood and stained glass — uplifting colour, not a bare room.
